page: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a Cloud GPU family server configured with two NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a total of 96 GB of VRAM. The system features an Intel x86_64 processor with 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs, operating at a ratio of one thread per core.

This server includes 375.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 11.72 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a 2200 GB NVMe SSD. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The CPU allocation for this instance is shared, which may introduce resource contention compared to dedicated CPU instances. Additionally, hardware flags for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), and cryptographic acceleration are not exposed on this instance type.

Given the dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs and large memory capacity, this configuration is designed for GPU-accelerated workloads such as machine learning inference, large language model execution, and parallel data processing tasks that require substantial VRAM and system memory.
description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a Cloud GPU server equipped with two NVIDIA L40S GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a total of 96 GB of VRAM. The host system features an Intel x86_64 processor with 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs under a shared CPU allocation model, with one thread per core. It is provisioned with 375.0 GB of system memory, translating to 11.72 GB per core, alongside a 2200 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. One complimentary public IPv4 address is included. Due to the shared CPU allocation and the absence of advanced CPU instruction sets like AVX or AMX, computational performance is heavily weighted toward the dual GPU accelerators. This hardware profile is suited for GPU-intensive workloads, including machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and data science tasks requiring significant VRAM and system memory.
og_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring two NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB VRAM, 32 Intel vCPUs, 375 GB RAM, and a 2200 GB NVMe SSD for GPU-accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a Cloud GPU server with two NVIDIA L40S GPUs, 96 GB VRAM, 32 Intel vCPUs, 375 GB RAM, and 2200 GB NVMe storage.
tagline: A dual-GPU server featuring ninety-six gigabytes of VRAM, substantial system memory, and local NVMe storage for parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs providing 96 GB of total VRAM.
32 Intel x86_64 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation.
375.0 GB of system memory and 2200 GB of NVMe SSD storage.
One thread per physical core without hyperthreading.
Designed for machine learning inference and GPU-accelerated data processing.
